political.s: Smucker's PAC contributed $280,840 and spent $470,000 on lobbying in 2024. Employee-affiliated contributions show mixed partisan patterns. The company maintains moderate political engagement focused on regulatory and trade issues relevant to food manufacturing.

charity.s: Smucker supports United Way, Red Cross disaster relief, community education programs, and local initiatives in manufacturing communities. The company operates matching gift programs and donated over 20 million meals in 2017. Charitable giving focuses on community resources and education.
charity.focus: Community resources, education, disaster relief, food security

environment.s: Smucker achieved 93% reduction in GHG emissions (Scope 1&2), surpassed 2020 waste diversion goals (96.2%), and established TRUE zero-waste certification targets by 2030. Company has renewable energy partnerships (122 MW wind) and sustainable sourcing programs for coffee and agriculture.

labor.s: Limited public NLRB violations found. Company faced some historical labor issues but maintains employee matching gifts and relief programs. CEO pay ratio of 133:1 (2024) is above average. Employee compensation ranked in top 20% for perks on Comparably.

dei.s: Smucker expanded DEI teams by 50%+ in 2023, operates employee resource groups, and promotes unconscious bias training. Company publicly commits to inclusive workplace culture reflecting consumer diversity. Board and leadership prioritize inclusion initiatives.

animals.s: Smucker states it does not conduct harmful experiments on animals, but uses third-party facilities for pet food palatability testing. The company uses dairy and eggs in consumer products. Historical pentobarbital contamination in pet food (2018-2020) from rendered ingredients raised animal welfare concerns.

guns.s: As a food and beverage company, Smucker has no involvement in firearms manufacturing or sales. Not applicable to business operations.

privacy.s: Smucker's privacy policy allows selling personal data for targeted advertising and behavioral tracking. Company collects cookies, uses Google Analytics, and shares data with service providers. Provides California/Canada privacy rights but practices are standard corporate data collection.

execPay.s: CEO Mark Smucker earned $10.37M in 2024 compensation (133:1 pay ratio). Compensation increased despite company unprofitability. Average executive compensation significantly below market average for comparable size companies.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
